mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL, suggests a benign economic environment without significant cost pressures, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at t-1mo is 278.88, indicating relatively stable prices
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat during periods of low and stable CPIAUCSL, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and returns is not statistically significant over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, which typically leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate healthy consumer demand and investment activity, signaling a strong economy that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns due to higher corporate revenues.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND HOUST > 1500k units (annualized)
failure_condition: When both RSAFS growth and HOUST meet the specified trigger conditions, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of occurrences over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Very low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) during a period of low inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicate highly accommodative monetary policy, which encourages risk-taking and boosts equity valuations by lowering discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: During periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL is stable, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are consistently negative or flat, or the correlation is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector, indicating broader economic weakness that negatively impacts corporate earnings and consequently S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.0% MoM for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances following two consecutive months of INDPRO declines exceeding 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in GDP (GDPC1) reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a strong fundamental backdrop for corporate profitability and thus drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence increases.
failure_condition: Over any 5-year rolling window, the correlation between GDPC1 growth and S&P 500 60-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as economic activity accelerates.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k jobs month-over-month over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a tightening monetary policy (FEDFUNDS increasing) signals an environment where the central bank is actively fighting inflation, which typically leads to higher discount rates and lower equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth > 0.4% MoM AND FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25pp in 3 months
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS increases meet the trigger conditions, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of occurrences over a 5-year backtest.
